de.uka.ilkd.key.java

Class Recoder2KeY

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.java.Recoder2KeY
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    JavaReader
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    SchemaRecoder2KeY

    public class Recoder2KeY
extends Object
implements JavaReader
    This class is the bridge between recoder ast data structures and KeY data structures. Syntactical entities and types can be transferred from recoder to KeY. It manages the entire contact with the recoder framework and ensures that their cross-referencing data is always uptodate. Prior to reading any source code, special classes (i.e. stubs for some needed library classes) are parsed in to have them available at any time. To use a Recoder2KeY bridge to convert data structures you can use the functions: readCompilationUnit(String), readCompilationUnitsAsFiles(String[]) or the readBlock(String, Context)-methods. Results are often stored in caches. It used to be monolithic but now uses separate classes for doing the actual conversion and type conversion.
    See Also:
    Recoder2KeYConverter, Recoder2KeYTypeConverter

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description
      private File bootClassPath
      the File object that describes the directory from which the internal classes are to be read.

      the set of File objects that describes the classpath to be searched for classes.

      the list of classnames that contain the classes that are referenced but not defined.

      this mapping stores the relation between recoder and KeY entities in a bidirectional way.

      this flag indicates whether we are currently parsing library classes (special classes)

    • Field Detail

      • classPath

        private List<File> classPath
        the set of File objects that describes the classpath to be searched for classes. it may contain a null file which indicates that the default classes are not to be read.

      • bootClassPath

        private File bootClassPath
        the File object that describes the directory from which the internal classes are to be read. They are read in differently - therefore the second category. A null value indicates that the boot classes are to be read from an internal repository.

      • mapping

        private KeYRecoderMapping mapping
        this mapping stores the relation between recoder and KeY entities in a bidirectional way. It is used for syntactical structures and types.

      • servConf

        private KeYCrossReferenceServiceConfiguration servConf
        Recoder's serviceConfiguration that is used throughout this process.

      • interactCounter

        private static int interactCounter
        counter used to enumerate the anonymous implicit classes

      • parsingLibs

        private boolean parsingLibs
        this flag indicates whether we are currently parsing library classes (special classes)

      • converter

        private Recoder2KeYConverter converter
        the object that handles the transformation from recoder AST to KeY AST

      • typeConverter

        private Recoder2KeYTypeConverter typeConverter
        the object that handles the transformation from recoder types to KeY types

      • dynamicallyCreatedCompilationUnits

        private Collection<? extends CompilationUnit> dynamicallyCreatedCompilationUnits
        the list of classnames that contain the classes that are referenced but not defined. For those classe types a dummy stub is created at parse time.

      • services

        private final Services services

    • Constructor Detail

      • Recoder2KeY

        public Recoder2KeY(Services services,
                   KeYCrossReferenceServiceConfiguration servConf,
                   KeYRecoderMapping rec2key,
                   NamespaceSet nss,
                   TypeConverter tc)
        create a new Recoder2KeY transformation object. The converter and type converter associated with this object will be created. Several properties of the recoder framework will be set up. The classpath is set to null, the mapping is retrieved from the services, as well as the underlying type converter
        Parameters:
        servConf - the service configuration to be used, not null
        rec2key - the mapping to store mapped types and mapped ASTs to, not null
        nss - the namespaces to work upon, not null
        tc - the type converter, not null

      • Recoder2KeY

        public Recoder2KeY(Services services,
                   NamespaceSet nss)
        create a new Recoder2KeY transformation object. The converter and type converter associated with this object will be created. Several properties of the recoder framework will be set up. The classpath is set to null, the mapping is retrieved from the services, as well as the underlying type converter
        Parameters:
        services - services to retrieve objects from, not null
        nss - the namespaces to work upon, not null

      • Recoder2KeY

        private Recoder2KeY(Services services,
                    KeYCrossReferenceServiceConfiguration servConf,
                    String classPath,
                    KeYRecoderMapping rec2key,
                    NamespaceSet nss,
                    TypeConverter tc)
        create a new Recoder2KeY transformation object. The converter and type converter associated with this object will be created. Several properties of the recoder framework will be set up.
        Parameters:
        servConf - the service configuration to be used, not null
        classPath - the classpath to look up source files, ignored if null
        rec2key - the mapping to store mapped types and mapped ASTs to, not null
        nss - the namespaces to work upon, not null
        tc - the type converter, not null
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if arguments are not valid (null e.g.)

                                             NamespaceSet nss)
        create the ast converter. This is overwritten in SchemaRecoder2KeY to use schema-aware converters.
        Parameters:
        services -
        nss - the namespaces provided to the constructor
        Returns:
        a newley created converter

      • readCompilationUnitsAsFiles

        public CompilationUnit[] readCompilationUnitsAsFiles(String[] cUnitStrings)
        parse a list of java files and transform it to the corresponding KeY entities. Each element of the array is treated as a filename to read in.
        Parameters:
        cUnitStrings - a list of strings, each element is interpreted as a file to be read. not null.
        Returns:
        a new list containing the recoder compilation units corresponding to the given files.

      • recoderCompilationUnitsAsFiles

        private List<CompilationUnit> recoderCompilationUnitsAsFiles(String[] cUnitStrings)
        parse a list of java files. Each element of the array is treated as a filename to read in.
        Parameters:
        cUnitStrings - a list of strings, each element is interpreted as a file to be read. not null.
        Returns:
        a new list containing the recoder compilation units corresponding to the given files.

      • readCompilationUnit

        public CompilationUnit readCompilationUnit(String cUnitString)
        read a compilation unit, given as a string.
        Parameters:
        cUnitString - a string represents a compilation unit
        Returns:
        a KeY structured compilation unit.

      • recoderCompilationUnits

        private List<CompilationUnit> recoderCompilationUnits(String[] cUnitStrings)
        read a number of compilation units, each given as a string.
        Parameters:
        cUnitStrings - an array of strings, each element represents a compilation unit
        Returns:
        a list of KeY structured compilation units.

      • setClassPath

        public void setClassPath(File bootClassPath,
                         List<File> classPath)

      • getDynamicallyCreatedClasses

        public List<String> getDynamicallyCreatedClasses()
        get the list of names of classes that have been created dynamically due to lacking definitions. For all classes that are referenced but not defined, an empty dummy stub is created. This method returns the list of their fully qualified class names.
        Returns:
        an unmodifiable list of fully qualified class names

      • parseInternalClasses

        private void parseInternalClasses(ProgramFactory pf,
                                  List<CompilationUnit> rcuList)
                           throws IOException,
                                  ParseException,
                                  ParserException
        This method loads the internal classes - also called the "boot" classes. If bootClassPath is set to null, it locates java classes that are stored internally within the jar-file or the binary directory. The JAVALANG.TXT file lists all files to be loaded. The files are found using a special JavaReduxFileCollection. If, however, bootClassPath is assigned a value, this is treated as a directory (not a JAR file at the moment) and all files in this directory are read in. This is done using a DirectoryFileCollection.
        Throws:
        IOException
        ParseException
        ParserException

      • parseLibs

        private List<CompilationUnit> parseLibs()
                                 throws ParseException,
                                        IOException,
                                        ParserException
        reads compilation units that will be treated as library classes. Proceed as follows:
        1. If "classPath" is set and contains at least one entry
          1. read every .java file within the entries (directories or zip files)
          2. read every .class file within the entries (directories or zip files)
        2. else read a special collection of classes that is stored internally
        Throws:
        ParserException
        IOException
        ParseException

      • removeCodeFromClasses

        private void removeCodeFromClasses(CompilationUnit rcu,
                                   boolean allowed)

      • parseSpecialClasses

        public void parseSpecialClasses()
        makes sure that the special classes (library classes) have been parsed in. If not parsed yet, the special classes are read in and converted. This method throws only runtime exceptions for historical reasons.

      • parseLibraryClasses0

        private void parseLibraryClasses0()
                           throws ParseException,
                                  IOException,
                                  ParserException
        Throws:
        ParseException
        IOException
        ParserException

      • transformModel

        protected void transformModel(List<CompilationUnit> cUnits)
        Transform a list of compilation units. Once a compilation unit has been parsed in and prior to converting it to the KeY structures, several transformations have to be performed upon it. You can add your own Transformation here. Make sure it is in the correct order.
        Parameters:
        cUnits - a list of compilation units, not null.

      • embedBlock

        protected MethodDeclaration embedBlock(StatementBlock block)
        wraps a RECODER StatementBlock in a method it is wrapped in a method called <virtual_method_for_parsing>.
        Parameters:
        block - the recoder.java.StatementBlock to wrap
        Returns:
        the enclosing recoder.java.MethodDeclaration

      • embedMethod

        protected ClassDeclaration embedMethod(MethodDeclaration mdecl,
                                       Context context)
        wraps a RECODER MethodDeclaration in a class
        Parameters:
        mdecl - the recoder.java.declaration.MethodDeclaration to wrap
        context - the recoder.java.declaration.ClassDeclaration where the method has to be embedded
        Returns:
        the enclosing recoder.java.declaration.ClassDeclaration

      • createEmptyContext

        public Context createEmptyContext()
        creates an empty RECODER compilation unit with a temporary name.
        Returns:
        the new recoder.java.CompilationUnit

      • createContext

        protected Context createContext(ImmutableList<ProgramVariable> pvs)
        create a new context with a temporary name and make a list of variables visible from within. Use the default source info.
        Parameters:
        pvs - a list of variables
        Returns:
        a newly created context.

      • createContext

        Parameters:

        csi - a special source info

        a newly created context.

        private VariableSpecification lookupVarSpec(ProgramVariable pv)
        look up in the mapping the variable specification for a program variable. used by addProgramVariablesToClassContext

      • name2typeReference

        private TypeReference name2typeReference(String typeName)
        given a name as string, construct a recoder type reference from it.
        Parameters:
        typeName - non-null type name as string
        Returns:
        a freshly created type reference to the given type.

      • recoderBlock

        StatementBlock recoderBlock(String block,
                            Context context)
        parses a given JavaBlock using the context to determine the right references and returns a statement block of recoder.
        Parameters:
        block - a String describing a java block
        context - recoder.java.CompilationUnit in which the block has to be interpreted
        Returns:
        the parsed and resolved recoder statement block

      • readBlock

        public JavaBlock readBlock(String block,
                           Context context)
        parses a given JavaBlock using the context to determine the right references
        Parameters:
        block - a String describing a java block
        context - recoder.java.CompilationUnit in which the block has to be interprested
        Returns:
        the parsed and resolved JavaBlock

      • readBlockWithEmptyContext

        public JavaBlock readBlockWithEmptyContext(String block)
        parses a given JavaBlock using the context to determine the right references using an empty context
        Specified by:
        readBlockWithEmptyContext in interface JavaReader
        Parameters:
        block - a String describing a java block
        Returns:
        the parsed and resolved JavaBlock

      • readBlockWithProgramVariables

        public JavaBlock readBlockWithProgramVariables(Namespace varns,
                                               String s)
        parses a given JavaBlock using a namespace to determine the right references using an empty context. The variables of the namespace are used to create a new class context
        Specified by:
        readBlockWithProgramVariables in interface JavaReader
        Parameters:
        s - a String describing a java block
        Returns:
        the parsed and resolved JavaBlock

      • interactClassDecl

        private ClassDeclaration interactClassDecl()
        make a new classdeclaration with a temporary name. The name is a unique implicit identifier.
        Returns:
        a newly created recoder ClassDeclaration with a unique name

      • extractPositionInfo

        private static int[] extractPositionInfo(String errorMessage)
        tries to parse recoders exception position information

      • reportError

        public static void reportError(String message,
                               Throwable t)
        report an error in form of a ConvertException. The cause is always attached to the resulting exception.
        Parameters:
        message - message to be used.
        t - the cause of the exceptional case
        Throws:
        ConvertException - always
